_comment: Llama-3.1-8B (32L, hidden 4096, 32 q-heads, 8 kv-heads, hd 128, 8B params) on a single H100-SXM (80GB, 3.35 TB/s HBM3). Single-request / low-concurrency inter-token latency ~11ms reported across vLLM/TensorRT-LLM benchmarks. Source: NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM + vLLM H100 benchmarks (see outputs/validation_vs_hardware.md). APPROXIMATE literature-derived anchor, bf16, short context.
name: llama8b_h100_b1_ctx1k

_comment: REMOVED in the honesty revision (audit finding): the former 'llama8b_h100_b32_ctx1k' point labeled 21ms as a BATCH-32 TPOT. That is physically inconsistent -- a dense-8B batch-32 decode on H100 is weight-read-bound at ~5ms (16GB/3.35TB/s), so 21ms implies ~1524 tok/s, not a clean batch-32 roofline point. The 21ms figure is really the contended/high-concurrency upper end of the SINGLE-STREAM ITL band, not a batch-32 measurement. Keeping it mislabeled dragged the fitted eta artificially down to ~0.40. Dropped rather than mislabel; replace with a real, instrumented batch-sweep point when available.
name: _removed_llama8b_h100_b32_ctx1k_see_comment

_comment: Llama-3.1-8B on H100, longer 8k context, single stream. ITL rises modestly with context (more KV to stream). Estimated ~13ms from the same benchmark family. APPROXIMATE.
name: llama8b_h100_b1_ctx8k
